what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to a specific board item in the capability-workshop channel.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
target is a board:// URI referencing item-f4c43898.
It has an id att-06f517ab and an added timestamp.
There are no code symbols present.
standouts: The target uses a board://item-f4c43898 URI.
The file is otherwise empty beyond frontmatter.
conventions: Follows an att- prefixed identifier scheme.
References a board item via a board:// URI scheme.
concerns: The attachment contains no payload or description beyond the link.
The referenced board item cannot be understood from this file alone.
The timestamp has microsecond precision, which may be generated rather than human-authored.
notes: A bare metadata stub that links a capability-workshop channel attachment to board item f4c43898.
questions: What content or discussion does board item f4c43898 represent?
Why does the attachment have no body or explanatory text?
Is the target board item expected to exist in the same project?
purpose_vs_actual: It functions as intended as a linker, but as an 'attachment' it carries no actual attached content.
intention_signals: To associate a channel attachment record with a specific board item in a capability workshop context.
novelty: Uses a board:// URI scheme to target a board item in attachment metadata.
contribution: Provides a persistent, file-local endpoint that links an attachment identifier to a board item identifier, which a fused system can use as a reference edge.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata file att-06f517ab in the capability-workshop channel references board item item-f4c43898 via a board:// URI and contains no additional content.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to attach or associate this channel record with a specific workshop board item.
